sourcingNotes.poolAssessment: Wine hub is the slot where Wikimedia Commons is strongest in a clean-licence direction for the Peninsula. The CSIRO ScienceImage collection on Commons includes multiple 1980s-era photographs of Pinot Noir and Chardonnay vines and fruit at Main Ridge Estate — an actual named Mornington Peninsula winery — all licensed CC-BY 3.0 with no SA contamination. These are almost-perfect Peninsula-verifiable vine images, but they are macro-scale (individual vines, individual bunches) rather than wide cellar-door landscapes. The editor needs to decide whether to lean into that specificity or wait for a wide vine-rows frame from Visit Victoria Content Hub.

candidates.licenseNotes: CC-BY 3.0 from CSIRO ScienceImage archive. Clean commercial use. Attribution: 'CSIRO ScienceImage / Wikimedia Commons (CC-BY-3.0)'. No SA, no ND, no NC.
candidates.sourcingAgentNote: Peninsula-verifiable Pinot Noir vines at Main Ridge Estate (one of the founding Mornington Peninsula wineries). CC-BY 3.0, no contamination. Strong editorial voice fit — cool-climate, specific, unglossed. However: the image is PORTRAIT orientation (3240x4320), which does not fit a 16:9 hero. A 16:9 crop is technically possible with the centre of the frame but loses ~40% of the image. Primary candidate IF the editor accepts a centre-crop derivative; otherwise defer to candidate B.

candidates.licenseNotes: CC-BY 3.0 from CSIRO ScienceImage archive. Same clean licence as candidate A.
candidates.place: Main Ridge Estate, Mornington Peninsula — Pinot Noir fruit
candidates.sourcingAgentNote: Landscape orientation at 4320x3240. Clean licence, specific Peninsula winery. Composition is tighter on the bunches and less sweeping than a cellar-door landscape. Editorial weight is the soft fail — this image reads as a specimen study, not a hero. Would make an excellent secondary or rhythm-break image on a wine article, but it is a compromise as a hub hero. Primary if the editor wants a landscape-native shape without the portrait-crop of candidate A.

candidates.place: Main Ridge Estate, Mornington Peninsula — Chardonnay fruit
candidates.sourcingAgentNote: Chardonnay version of candidate B. Same editorial-weight tension. Kept in the shortlist so the editor can choose between Pinot Noir (which owns the regional narrative) and Chardonnay (which is the rising-quality Peninsula story from 2020 onwards). Editorially Pinot is probably the right call for a hub hero; Chardonnay would be a better feature image for a Chardonnay-specific journal piece.

editorActionsRequested.question: Candidate A is portrait and needs a centre crop to become 16:9. Candidate B is native landscape but specimen-study in energy. Pick A with crop, B as-is, or defer the wine hub hero until Visit Victoria Content Hub gives us a wide cellar-door-at-dawn frame.
editorActionsRequested.defaultIfNoReply: Defer to Visit Victoria Content Hub unlock.
